title: Neoregelia concentrica
taxonomy: Neoregelia concentrica is a species of flowering plant in the family Bromeliaceae. It is a type of bromeliad, which is a group of tropical plants characterized by their distinctive leaves and ability to form rosettes. Bromeliads are further divided into several genera, with Neoregelia being one of the most common and widely cultivated. Neoregelia concentrica is native to the tropical regions of Central and South America, where it grows in the understory of rainforests. This plant is often grown as an ornamental for its vibrant flowers and attractive foliage.

Traditionally, the plant has been used in folk medicine to treat various ailments, including digestive issues and inflammation. Some studies suggest that the plant's compounds may have antibacterial and antifungal properties, which could contribute to its medicinal potential. However, further investigation is necessary to fully understand its benefits and potential side effects. More research is required to confirm the specific medicinal uses of Neoregelia concentrica.

intro: Neoregelia concentrica, also known as the "Concentric Bromeliad," is a terrestrial bromeliad plant species native to South America, characterized by its bright, colorful flowers and unique, concentric ring patterns. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its purported health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of digestive issues, wounds, and respiratory infections. The bioactive constituents of Neoregelia concentrica include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with this plant include infusions and decoctions, as well as extracts used in topical creams and ointments to support skin health and wound healing.
